description: Scene validator benchmark — 25 cases (10 good, 10 bad, 5 ambiguous)

cases.label: PASS
cases.title: The Street Cat
cases.summary: A musician plays guitar while a stray cat harmonizes on his amp
cases.reason: Clear hook (scene 1-2), payoff (scene 5), understandable silently, good pacing

cases.label: PASS
cases.title: The Returning Book
cases.summary: A bookshop owner discovers a sold book keeps reappearing on the shelf
cases.reason: Strong hook (mysterious empty slot), escalation, clear payoff (acceptance/smile)

cases.label: PASS
cases.title: The Paper Crane
cases.summary: A child folds a paper crane that catches the wind and lands on a lonely man's bench
cases.reason: Visual journey, no dialogue needed, hook (folding), payoff (connection)

cases.label: PASS
cases.title: The Baker's Mistake
cases.summary: A baker accidentally creates a lopsided cake that becomes the most popular item
cases.reason: Comedy arc, visual punchline, clear without audio

cases.label: PASS
cases.title: The Shadow Match
cases.summary: Two strangers realize their shadows are holding hands on the wall
cases.reason: Visual hook (shadows), surprise, payoff (connection)

cases.label: PASS
cases.title: The Raindrop Race
cases.summary: A child watches two raindrops race down a window, cheering for one
cases.reason: Simple, universal, visual, no audio needed

cases.label: PASS
cases.title: The Lost Key
cases.summary: A woman finds a key on a park bench and discovers it opens a nearby music box
cases.reason: Mystery hook, discovery arc, satisfying payoff

cases.label: PASS
cases.title: The Sleeping Dog
cases.summary: A dog's legs twitch in sleep, dreaming of chasing a squirrel seen earlier
cases.reason: Relatable, visual comedy, clear callback

cases.label: PASS
cases.title: The Flower Pot
cases.summary: A wilting flower straightens up when a child sings to it
cases.reason: Magical realism, visual transformation, heartwarming payoff

cases.label: PASS
cases.title: The Mirror Wave
cases.summary: A man waves at himself in a store window and his reflection waves back late
cases.reason: Surprise twist, visual-only humor, strong hook

cases.label: FAIL
cases.title: The Complex Journey
cases.summary: A woman travels across three countries to find her grandmother's recipe
cases.reason: Too many locations for 24s, confusing without context, needs narration to follow

cases.label: FAIL
cases.title: The Conversation
cases.summary: Two friends discuss their relationship problems over coffee
cases.reason: Entirely dialogue-dependent, no visual story, redundant talking scenes

cases.label: FAIL
cases.title: The One Scene
cases.summary: A sunset over the ocean
cases.reason: Only 1 scene, no narrative, no hook, no payoff

cases.label: FAIL
cases.title: The Repetition
cases.summary: A man opens doors
cases.reason: All scenes are identical beat, no progression, no payoff

cases.label: FAIL
cases.title: The Lecture
cases.summary: A professor explains quantum physics on a whiteboard
cases.reason: Not understandable without audio, no visual story, too long at 20s for repetitive content

cases.label: FAIL
cases.title: The Abstract
cases.summary: A meditation on the meaning of time through symbolic imagery
cases.reason: Too abstract, no character, no narrative arc, no hook/payoff

cases.label: FAIL
cases.title: No Payoff Story
cases.summary: A cat walks across a room
cases.reason: No hook, no tension, no payoff, just linear motion

cases.label: FAIL
cases.title: The Marathon
cases.summary: A complete marathon from start to finish
cases.reason: Too many scenes, too short each, pacing too fast, no emotional arc

cases.label: FAIL
cases.title: The Internal Monologue
cases.summary: A person thinks about their life choices while staring out a window
cases.reason: Requires narration, no visual progression, too slow, runtime 25s with no action

cases.label: FAIL
cases.title: The Confusing Cut
cases.summary: Random scenes that don't connect
cases.reason: No narrative connection between scenes, confusing sequence

cases.label: AMBIGUOUS
cases.title: The Loop
cases.summary: A person walks in a circle and ends up where they started
cases.reason: Could be clever (loop reveal) or boring (just walking), depends on execution

cases.label: AMBIGUOUS
cases.title: The Gift
cases.summary: Someone wraps a gift, gives it, and the recipient opens it
cases.reason: Technically complete arc but generic, hook depends on what's inside

cases.label: AMBIGUOUS
cases.title: The Long Take
cases.summary: A timelapse of a flower blooming and wilting
cases.reason: Visually beautiful but no character, no hook beyond aesthetic. Could work as ASMR/art but not as story

cases.label: AMBIGUOUS
cases.title: The Copy Cat
cases.summary: A cat copies everything its owner does
cases.reason: Cute comedy but scenes 1-4 are repetitive (same beat twice). Scene 6 is a payoff but weak

cases.label: AMBIGUOUS
cases.title: The Umbrella
cases.summary: A person shares their umbrella with a stranger in the rain
cases.reason: Sweet but predictable. Hook is weak (rain is common), payoff is expected. Could pass if execution is charming
